- verify-all: Run the full check + clippy sweep across the workspace AND the WASM plugin (panopt-zellij). Use after non-trivial edits, before declaring work done, or when asked to verify the repo builds cleanly. Captures the dual-target dance (workspace builds for host, panopt-zellij builds only for wasm32-wasip1) so you don't have to remember the right manifest-path/target each time.
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

input.description: Find startup delay before todo list
input.prompt:
In the panopt repo (/home/gregz/cp/panopt), there is a startup delay of approximately 2 seconds before the todo list is shown in the right pane when starting up. I need to find where this delay is configured/implemented.

Search the codebase for:
1. Any sleep/delay/timeout values around 2 seconds (e.g. `Duration::from_secs(2)`, `2000` ms, `sleep`, `delay`) particularly related to startup, showing panes, or the right pane / todo list.
2. The code path that shows the todo list in the right pane on startup — likely in `crates/panopt/src/up.rs` or related launcher code, or possibly in the Zellij plugin `crates/panopt-zellij/`.
3. Any constants or config related to this delay.

Report back: the exact file path(s) and line number(s) where this 2-second delay is set, the surrounding code context, and how the value is used. Be thorough — check both the `panopt` launcher crate and the `panopt-zellij` plugin crate. Report relevant code excerpts.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a complete picture. Let me create a summary report by combining all findings:

## Summary

I found the 2-second startup delay in the panopt codebase. Here are the details:

### Location and Implementation

**File:** `/home/gregz/cp/panopt/crates/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs`

**Lines involved:**
- **Line 535**: Field definition - `initial_preview_delay: u32,` (part of the `PanoptPane` struct)
- **Line 678**: Timer initialization in `new()` - `set_timeout(1.0);`
- **Lines 703-710**: The delay counter and trigger logic in the `update()` method

### How It Works

The delay is implemented as a tick counter on a 1-second timer:

1. **Line 678** (in `new()`): When the plugin initializes on startup, it calls `set_timeout(1.0)` to arm a 1-second timer.

2. **Lines 703-710** (in `update()` -> `Event::Timer` handler): 
   - Every 1 second (when the timer fires), the counter `initial_preview_delay` is incremented by 1
   - When the counter reaches **2 or higher** (meaning at least 2 timer ticks, ~2 seconds), the initial preview is triggered
   - This happens only in the **Todos pane** (`if self.mode == Mode::Todos`)
   - The preview shows the first todo item in the right pane by calling `self.preview_cursor()`
   - After the preview is shown, `initial_preview_done` is set to `true` so this only happens once per cockpit boot

3. **Line 721**: The timer is continuously re-armed with `set_timeout(1.0)` so the plugin receives periodic timer events

### Context

The comment at lines 695-697 explains the purpose:
```
// Only the Todos pane drives the initial preview - one preview
// per cockpit boot is enough, and the other panes have nothing
// useful to preview until the user navigates them.
```

This delay gives the Zellij UI time to fully initialize before showing the todo list in the right pane. The `initial_preview_done` boolean flag (line 533) ensures this only executes once during startup.

### Related Code

The `preview_cursor()` function (line 1548) actually performs the preview action - it opens the focused todo item in the right pane viewer.

content.text: Let me read the exact code to understand the mechanism precisely before planning.

I now have a complete understanding. The delay is a tick counter: a 1-second timer fires repeatedly, incrementing `initial_preview_delay`, and the preview triggers once the counter reaches `>= 2` (≈2 seconds). Changing the threshold to `>= 1` makes it ≈1 second. This is a trivial one-line change, so I'll write the plan directly.

input.content:
# Plan — Todo #23: Adjust startup delay before showing the todo list in the right pane

## Context

On cockpit startup, the Zellij sidebar plugin waits ~2 seconds before auto-previewing the first todo in the right pane. The user finds 2 seconds "a lifetime" and wants to try 1 second.

The delay is not a literal sleep — it's a tick counter driven by a repeating 1-second timer. The first `set_timeout(1.0)` is armed in `new()`; each `Event::Timer` increments `initial_preview_delay`, and the initial preview fires once the counter reaches the threshold. With the threshold at `>= 2`, the preview lands on the second tick (~2 s). Lowering the threshold to `>= 1` makes it fire on the first tick (~1 s).

## Change

**File:** `crates/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs` (line 704, inside the `Event::Timer` arm)

```rust
// before
if self.initial_preview_delay >= 2 {
// after
if self.initial_preview_delay >= 1 {
```

That is the only code edit. No struct field, timer interval, or other logic changes are needed.

## Verification

1. Rebuild the WASM plugin artifact: `just plugin-release`.
2. Reload runtime artifacts so the running cockpit picks up the new plugin (`just refresh`, or `just stop && just up` plus reloading the Zellij session — the plugin runs as a pane subprocess, so a plain daemon restart is not enough).
3. Start the cockpit fresh and confirm the first todo appears in the right pane after ~1 second instead of ~2.
4. Sanity-check the plugin still builds clean: `cargo check --manifest-path crates/panopt-zellij/Cargo.toml --target wasm32-wasip1` (covered by the `verify-all` skill).

## Notes

- This is plugin-only; `panoptd`/`panopt` host crates are untouched.
- If 1 second still feels too slow/fast, the same single value is the knob to turn.

content.content: File created successfully at: /home/gregz/.claude/plans/plan-23-resilient-ullman.md (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

Done. Todo #23 is complete.

**Change:** `crates/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs` — lowered the initial-preview tick threshold from `>= 2` to `>= 1` in the `Event::Timer` arm. The preview now fires on the first 1-second tick instead of the second, so the todo list appears in the right pane after ~1 second instead of ~2.

**Verified:**
- `cargo check` (wasm32-wasip1) — clean
- `just plugin-release` — built the optimized artifact

**To see it live:** the running cockpit is still using the old plugin subprocess. Reload it with `just refresh` (or `just stop && just up` plus reloading the Zellij session) and start fresh to confirm the ~1s timing feels right. If 1 second still isn't quite the sweet spot, that same threshold is the single knob to turn.
